message: 🌱 Add caddy: Strip path for reverse proxied requests mission (#467)

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,76 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "caddy-3266-strip-path-for-reverse-proxied-requests",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "caddy: Strip path for reverse proxied requests",
+    "description": "Strip path for reverse proxied requests. This issue affects 13+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify caddy feature symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your caddy deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=caddy
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=caddy -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: Strip path for reverse proxied requests"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Check current resource configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant caddy resources:
```bash
kubectl get resource -A
kubectl describe resource <name> -n <namespace>
```
The [`reverse_proxy` directive](https://caddyserver.com/docs/caddyfile/directives/reverse_proxy) does not strip the path of the request before forwarding the request upstream. For example, in the foll"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Strip path for reverse proxied requests",
+        "description": "Implementation of https://github.com/caddyserver/caddy/issues/3266

~This is incomplete/non-functional, still has debug prints etc. The general idea is there but it's not working as expected.~

Edit: Figured it out! Didn't realize I should be using `h.NewRoute()` which does the trick.

Caddyfile:
``
```yaml
example.com {
  route /foo* {
    reverse_proxy upstream:80
  }
}
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm Strip path for reverse proxied requests is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=caddy -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "The root cause is: Implementation of https://github.com/caddyserver/caddy/issues/3266

~This is incomplete/non-functional, still has debug prints etc. The general idea is there but it's not working as expected.~

Edit: Figured it out! Didn't realize I should be using `h.NewRoute()` which does the trick.

Caddyfile:
```
:80

handle_path /api/v1 {
    respond "API v1"
}

file_server
```

JSON:
```
{
  "apps": {
    "h. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "example.com {
  route /foo* {
    reverse_proxy upstream:80
  }
}",
+        "example.com {
  route /foo* {
    uri strip_prefix /foo
    reverse_proxy upstream:80
  }
}",
+        "example.com {
  route /api {
    route /v1* {
      uri strip_prefix /api/v1
      reverse_proxy oldapi:80
    }

    route /v2* {
      uri strip_prefix /api/v1
      reverse_proxy newapi:80
    }
  }

  route /docs* {
    uri strip_prefix /docs
    reverse_proxy docs:80
  }

  reverse_proxy website:80
}"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
